message.content: Adversaries may stage data collected from multiple systems in a central location on one system before exfiltration, a technique known as Remote Data Staging. This method involves using interactive command shells and common functionalities within cmd and bash to copy data into a staging location. In cloud environments, adversaries may stage data within a specific instance or virtual machine. This approach minimizes the number of connections to the Command and Control (C2) server, aiding in evasion. Notable groups like APT28, APT29, and FIN6 have employed this technique, often using password-protected archives or designated servers within the target environment. Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unusual data aggregation activities, implementing strict access controls, and employing network segmentation to limit the movement of data within the environment. Regular audits and anomaly detection systems can also help identify and prevent unauthorized data staging activities.
